skill.name: reasoning-causal
skill.description: Guides evidence-based business decisions through a staged causal reasoning workflow from observation to learning.
skill.summary: Structure operational decisions with input capture, hypothesis testing, impact analysis, action planning, and learning updates.
skill.author: BellaBe

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many external command and weak cryptography patterns, but sampled evidence shows Markdown code fences, inline paths, and business templates rather than executable code. No confirmed malware, prompt injection, network exfiltration, or real shell execution was found. The remaining risk is that the skill instructs agents to create and update operational files and may allow autonomous business decisions, so publication should include a filesystem and human-review warning.

medium_findings.title: Agent-Directed Filesystem Updates
medium_findings.description: The skill asks agents to create thread directories and stage files, then update Canvas and goal records. This is legitimate for an operations workflow, but users should review workspace changes before applying them to important strategy files. Confidence: 0.74. Confidence reasoning: The instructions explicitly name files and updates, but they are Markdown workflow artifacts, not hidden code execution.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instructions explicitly name files and updates, but they are Markdown workflow artifacts, not hidden code execution.

medium_findings.title: Autonomous Business Decision Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill defines autonomous AI decision paths and an override where AI may proceed despite high impact when several criteria are met. This is a governance risk for business operations, not a malware indicator, and should require user review for high-impact commitments. Confidence: 0.66. Confidence reasoning: The behavior is stated in workflow guidance, but actual authority depends on the host agent and user permissions.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The behavior is stated in workflow guidance, but actual authority depends on the host agent and user permissions.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Reported as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The external command detections are Markdown inline code and fenced examples for paths, file names, templates, and pseudo-code. I did not find evidence that these files execute Ruby backticks, spawn shell commands, or run user-controlled commands. Confidence: 0.90. Confidence reasoning: Representative flagged lines are in Markdown documentation and examples, with no executable source file present in the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Representative flagged lines are in Markdown documentation and examples, with no executable source file present in the skill.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Detections Are Business Terms and Templates
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography detections occur in planning templates and business examples that discuss technical design, branding, ROI, or confidence. No evidence found of MD5, SHA-1, DES, or other weak cryptographic implementation being executed by the skill. Confidence: 0.86. Confidence reasoning: Sampled high-severity lines are Markdown template text or business examples, not runnable cryptographic code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Sampled high-severity lines are Markdown template text or business examples, not runnable cryptographic code.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Reconnaissance Terms Are Workflow Examples
low_findings.description: System and network reconnaissance detections appear in operational planning language such as links, affected services, channels, and status tracking. No evidence found of network scanning, host enumeration, credential collection, or data exfiltration instructions. Confidence: 0.82. Confidence reasoning: Sampled locations describe business evidence links and operational dependencies rather than commands or network probes.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Sampled locations describe business evidence links and operational dependencies rather than commands or network probes.

content.value_statement: Business teams often mix facts, assumptions, decisions, and outcomes in one discussion. This skill separates each step into a traceable causal workflow that supports better execution and learning.
actual_capabilities: Defines a six-stage flow from input capture through learning updates.
Provides templates for observations, hypotheses, implications, decisions, actions, and outcomes.
Links decisions to business assumptions, impact scores, goals, and Canvas updates.
Separates operational thread types for sales, marketing, engineering, and business decisions.
Includes guidance for human review when impact or risk is high.
limitations: It is a Markdown guidance skill and does not include executable automation.
Users must supply accurate business data, evidence links, costs, and outcomes.
High-impact decisions still need human review and organizational approval.
The workflow assumes a Canvas and goal-tracking structure exists in the workspace.

use_cases.title: Validate a Product Assumption
use_cases.description: Turn customer feedback into a hypothesis, quantify impact, and decide whether to build or defer.

use_cases.title: Coordinate Operational Execution
use_cases.description: Break an approved decision into assigned actions with owners, deadlines, dependencies, and success criteria.

use_cases.title: Close the Learning Loop
use_cases.description: Compare expected and actual outcomes, update assumptions, and record follow-up opportunities.

prompt_templates.title: Capture an Observation
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner: start a new causal thread from one factual signal.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use reasoning-causal to create Stage 1 for this observation: [paste fact]. Keep interpretation and recommendations out of the input.

prompt_templates.title: Test a Business Assumption
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate: connect evidence to an existing Canvas assumption.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use reasoning-causal Stage 2 to map this observation to our assumptions. Identify what is validated, challenged, or newly hypothesized.

prompt_templates.title: Quantify Decision Impact
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate: compare costs, revenue, risk, and timelines.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use reasoning-causal Stages 3 and 4 for this proposed decision. Include alternatives, ROI, approval threshold, and human review status.

prompt_templates.title: Run a Full Decision Thread
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced: produce the complete staged workflow and learning plan.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use reasoning-causal to build a full thread for [initiative]. Create stages for input, hypothesis, implication, decision, actions, and learning.

output_examples.input: A major customer asked for a premium deployment option and named a target annual budget.
output_examples.output: The skill produces a factual input record, links it to pricing and segment assumptions, estimates revenue and cost, then proposes a decision gate.

output_examples.input: A conversion metric dropped after a platform change.
output_examples.output: The skill separates the metric observation from interpretation, creates a testable hypothesis, and outlines impact scenarios before recommending action.

output_examples.input: An initiative finished and the team needs to update strategy documents.
output_examples.output: The skill summarizes expected versus actual outcomes, validates assumptions, identifies surprises, and lists Canvas or goal updates.

best_practices: Start with evidence and dates before asking for conclusions or recommendations.
Require human approval for decisions with high cost, high risk, or strategic impact.
Review generated file changes before committing updates to strategy or goal documents.
anti_patterns: Do not skip from customer feedback directly to action planning.
Do not treat estimates as facts without naming assumptions and confidence levels.
Do not allow autonomous execution for commitments that need legal, financial, or executive approval.

faq.question: What does this skill help me do?
faq.answer: It structures business decisions into evidence, hypothesis, impact, decision, action, and learning stages.

faq.question: Does it run commands or scripts?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed skill is Markdown guidance and templates. It does not include executable scripts.

faq.question: Can it make decisions without me?
faq.answer: It includes autonomous decision guidance, but high-impact choices should be reviewed by a human owner.

faq.question: What files can it create?
faq.answer: It guides creation of thread folders and Markdown stage files for decisions, actions, and learning records.

faq.question: Who is the best user for this skill?
faq.answer: It fits founders, product managers, operators, and teams that use structured strategy documents.

faq.question: What should I prepare before using it?
faq.answer: Prepare the observation, supporting evidence, known assumptions, estimated costs, expected impact, and approval rules.
